posts.title: Why Group Wellness Rituals Work Better Than Solo Self-Care
posts.excerpt: Self-care has become an individual sport. The wellness industry has sold it to you as a solo pursuit: your morning routine, your meditation cushion, your solo run, your quiet cup of coffee before the world wakes up. And all of that is fine. But the research on what actually sustains wellness behaviors over time points in a different direction. The people who maintain healthy habits for decades are almost never doing it alone.

posts.title: Thriving Decades: How to Think About Aging That Keeps You Active and Engaged
posts.excerpt: The wellness industry talks about aging like it is a disease to be delayed. Anti-aging creams, age-defying supplements, age reversal clinics charging 20k for treatments that do not work. Meanwhile, the actual research on what predicts healthy aging has been staring us in the face for decades. It is not about reversing the clock. It is about maintaining the capabilities that make life worth living.

posts.title: The Real Cost of Decision Fatigue: Why You Make Worse Decisions All Day
posts.excerpt: You made 200 decisions before 9 AM. What to wear, what to eat, whether to answer that email or wait, the temperature on the thermostat, the route to work. By the time you get to the important decision, you have already spent most of your decision-making budget. This is not a productivity problem. This is a biology problem.
